0x913af9a61d1a59aa5d21ce9bbf7fd44ed61db4.name: Founder @ OPSEK
0x913af9a61d1a59aa5d21ce9bbf7fd44ed61db4.motivation:
I am fully committed to improving the security of the ecosystem. I am totally convinced that security is the biggest issue the industry is facing right now, stopping it from achieving mass adoption. If we continue on this path, with DPRK being funded by large hacks like Bybit, we are gonna be a total failure.

I know for sure that my knowledge and experience will be valuable in order to enhance the security of Arbitrum’s Security Council, infrastructure, team and community.

I am fully doxxed and dedicate lots of resources to talk about the importance of Security in the Web3 ecosystem from my Twitter account with more than 83K followers, with the Blockchain Security Series podcast and with lots of free Blockchain security courses you can check at at Defy Education Youtube Channel.

I know how security councils work and what they expect from signers and I can fulfil all those requirements.
0x913af9a61d1a59aa5d21ce9bbf7fd44ed61db4.qualifications:
I am Pablo Sabbatella, also known as pablito.eth. I am a web3 operational security researcher, member of SEAL (Security Alliance) and I am applying for the security council as founder of Opsek. I also created “Blockchain Security series” podcast.

I started with cybersecurity back in 1999, when I founded Hackemate and have been involved in technology since then. I am part of the Optimism Security Council and the Polygon Protocol Council.

I founded Opsek, where we do operational security audits and training for Web3 organizations (DeFi, CEXs, L1s, L2s, VCs, service providers and HNWI). The reason behind my work and founding Opsek is very simple: 99% of funds being lost are due to operational security issues (Private key leakage, malware, exploits, social engineering, phishing, account takeovers, domain hijacking, etc) and not due to smart contract hacks anymore.
My expertise is understanding an organization, defining and protecting its attack surface: what does the organization do? Who is the team? What are the tools and tack that they use? What does the day to day operation look like? What are the most valuable assets it’s protecting? Which are the biggest risks? We also train the teams on physical security.
Part of our auditing process includes multisigs: how were they created? Who are the signers? What’s the appropriate threshold? How are private keys generated? How are seeds handled? Are they backed up or deleted? How do you travel with your hardware wallet? Have signers developed a threat model? Hardware wallet diversity, frontend diversity, transactions verification and simulation, definition of procedures and policies, etc.

I have already audited many firms (many of them we do not make public). Some of them: Optimism, Sky (ex MakerDao), Centrifuge, Contango, Midas, Aligned Layer, and many more.
I have participated in many war rooms, incident response and helped many people and companies save funds during attacks (and still do this daily).

Some of my presentations:

- TOTP apps are dead and why you are doing 2FA wrong @ darkMode (Denver - 02/2026)
https://darkmode.securityalliance.org/darkmode-2026/talk/CYHTKN/

- Professionals hack people, not systems @ DeFi Security Summit (Bangkok - 11/2024)
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1ZQIDkEfY5w

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9KFyJiyjxfE

- Apple Stack Hardening: Security Essentials for macOS, iOS & AppleID @ DeFi Security Summit (Buenos Aires - 11/2025)
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jgc4z_gQ6vg

- OpSec for the Dark Forest (or how to avoid getting rekt) @ Devcon 7 (Bangkok - 11/2024)
https://archive.devcon.org/devcon-7/opsec-for-the-dark-forest-or-how-to-avoid-getting-rekt/

- Operational security in Web3: a review of major OpSec incidents @ DSS Webinars (Online - 04/2025)
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GuQXUyMDd_s

- Physical and Operational Security 101 @ Ethereum Community Conference 8 (Cannes - 07/2025)
https://ethcc.io/agenda/physical-and-operational-security-101

- How to securely configure and use Telegram & Twitter @ Ethereum Community Conference 7 (Brussels - 07/2024)

Projects I created but where I am not involved anymore:
- I co-founded Ethereum Argentina.
- I created the first “Blockchain and DeFi" subject in an Argentinian University and served as teacher for two years.
- I founded Defy Education.

Disclosure: I am an active signer in the Optimism Security Council and the Polygon Protocol Council. Both of them do not have conflicts of interest.

0xbbd2e01efb88ce00f8f5b6b9a6969660700893.name: Solutions Eng Head @ Turnkey
0xbbd2e01efb88ce00f8f5b6b9a6969660700893.motivation:
Security councils serve as the final line of defense for DAOs, necessitating a membership that is highly responsive, technically elite, and deeply governance-aware. My tenure on various councils and my work across the DAO security landscape has given me a front-row seat to the evolving threats facing decentralized protocols. I am acutely aware of the delicate challenge in balancing technical risk with governance capture incentives and the complex legal concerns that surround decentralized infrastructure. This specific awareness allows me to move beyond being just a signer to being a proactive contributor to the council’s strategic voice.

I am personally driven by the mission of proving that decentralized public good systems can be made secure through appropriate, expert stewardship. My experience makes me a unique candidate capable of bridging the gap between high-level governance and granular technical security. Having established working relationships with many of the current council members and candidates, I am positioned to be a highly collaborative participant who can maintain independent decision-making in high-stress situations. My focus is on ensuring transparent, security-first decision-making and collaborating with the broader DAO to fortify Arbitrum’s infrastructure. I believe the Security Council and its members should have a more active, expert voice in specific areas of the DAO where technical and constitutional risks intersect.
0xbbd2e01efb88ce00f8f5b6b9a6969660700893.qualifications:
I have spent my career at the intersection of smart contract security, governance, and incident response, securing major DeFi protocols and DAOs. Currently, I serve as Head of Solutions Engineering at Turnkey, leading secure wallet infrastructure and private key management for major web3 protocols, dapps, and financial institutions. Previously, I was Head of Solutions Architecture at OpenZeppelin, where I designed security frameworks for DAOs and co-authored the Security Council Best Practices guide. I also served as Head of Solutions Engineering at Blockaid, leading security integrations for teams like Coinbase, MetaMask, and Uniswap.

I have been a Security Advisor for the Compound Foundation for over three years, where I recently managed a $2M security RFP and serve on the Community Multi-sig. This role is directly relevant to Arbitrum, as Compound currently has approximately $900M TVL deployed on the network. I bring a battle-tested background to the council, having led or contributed to emergency responses for complex incidents, including the Compound TUSD Integration Issue, which had $80M at risk, the ThirdWeb address spoofing disclosure, and the Compound cETH price feed response.

Over the past year, I have been an active contributor to Arbitrum as a Security Council Member and as a member of the Stylus Sprint Grants Committee through January 2026. I also provided direct feedback on the forums regarding the Security Council Election Process Improvements to help refine the process for future cohorts. My previous contributions to the ecosystem include leading OpenZeppelin’s early ARDC work over a year ago. I am an active contributor to the Security Alliance Technical Council and have served on security councils for Story Protocol, Compound, and zkSync.
0xbbd2e01efb88ce00f8f5b6b9a6969660700893.projectsInvolved: Turnkey (Solutions Eng Head), Compound (Advisor & Multi-sig Member), Story (Security Council Member)

0x06f2cafb5276b11baa6679abe49d5a5c0a911f.name: Head of Ecosystem @ CertiK
0x06f2cafb5276b11baa6679abe49d5a5c0a911f.motivation: I am a security maxi who believes that doing everything we can to prevent hacks and security incidents within our ecosystem is the only way to truly hit mass adoption. Arbitrum is one of the more important ecosystems within the web3 space and needs highly skilled, proficient people to be on the Security Council. I have the skills, experience, and availability to contribute in this way to the Arbitrum community.
0x06f2cafb5276b11baa6679abe49d5a5c0a911f.qualifications: I have over a decade of experience in the blockchain space with much of that time being spent in security roles. During my time at the Ethereum Foundation (2016-2021) I coordinated every network upgrade and was on the emergency response team responsible for responding to security events on chain and off chains. This gave me a lot of experience in war rooms, emergency comms, and security councils. I was a member of the Polygon PoS security council multi-sig while I worked there from 2023-2025. I am currently on the board of directions for the Ethereum Cat Herders Institute with the role of Treasurer. Because of this and prior roles, I have a lot of experiences with Safe.
0x06f2cafb5276b11baa6679abe49d5a5c0a911f.projectsInvolved: CertiK, Ethereum Cat Herders Institute, Akasha (contractor), Status

0xe1197e2ab23ee33c99ec91f17198d5264fc7df.name: Cofounder/CTO - Hexens
0xe1197e2ab23ee33c99ec91f17198d5264fc7df.motivation:
Arbitrum is at an inflection point. The Stylus upgrade significantly expands the smart contract execution surface beyond Solidity into Rust and C++, introducing vulnerability classes that most existing council members and auditors have limited exposure to. My background in low-level systems security, VM internals, vulnerability research and reverse engineering is directly relevant to evaluating risks at this layer.

Separately, having built Glider, a purpose-built tool for real-time smart contract vulnerability scanning that can accelerate blast radius assessment in an emergency scenario. This has already been demonstrated in production during the Thirdweb, Balancer and SOLC bug incidents.

My approval on the Arbitrum Audit Program means this candidacy is part of a broader, longer-term commitment to the ecosystem.
0xe1197e2ab23ee33c99ec91f17198d5264fc7df.qualifications:
The Arbitrum Security Council’s core function is emergency response. Due to the nature of our tools (and not only), I also have direct, hands-on experience in one of the biggest cases, like:
Thirdweb hack: Cooperated with chains and used Glider to determine the blast radius and help identify all affected projects
Balancer hack: scanned the whole integrated chains (using the same tools) to identify affected contracts and proactively notified projects before they were exploited within 15 minutes after the hack became public.
Solidity bug case - again, Glider was used to search for affected projects, allowing us to proactively notify them before they were exploited (more in the section below).
Relevant Credentials & Affiliations
Member of the wider Security Alliance (SEAL) community.
Member of the Polygon and Bitcoin security councils
Hexens is an approved auditor on the Arbitrum Audit Program
Competitive Security Background - I am a winner of over 20 international cybersecurity competitions (CTFs), both solo and as a member of the MSLC team.
0xe1197e2ab23ee33c99ec91f17198d5264fc7df.projectsInvolved:
Member of the wider Security Alliance (SEAL) community.
Member of the Polygon and Bitcoin security councils.
Hexens is an approved auditor on the Arbitrum Audit Program. 
I am not aware of any conflicts of interest that would impair my ability to act in the best interests of the Arbitrum DAO as a Security Council member. If any arise during my tenure, I will disclose them promptly and recuse myself where appropriate.

0xef7b13326c7c680735d792b63587096cf1b273.name: Web3 Sec. Engineer (exTOB)
0xef7b13326c7c680735d792b63587096cf1b273.motivation:
The Security Council plays a critical role in protecting the protocol during emergency actions and governance-approved upgrades. Effective decisions require both deep familiarity with the system and strong technical judgment when evaluating security-sensitive situations.

One area I believe will become increasingly important is the use of AI-assisted security tooling and agent-based analysis to strengthen smart contract and protocol security for the Arbitrum infrastructure. Recent advances in AI systems are making it possible to augment traditional auditing techniques with automated reasoning, vulnerability discovery, and continuous monitoring. If used carefully, these approaches could significantly improve our ability to detect and prevent vulnerabilities in complex systems like Arbitrum and Stylus.

As a member of the Security Council, I would advocate for exploring and leveraging state-of-the-art security techniques, including emerging AI-based approaches, while remaining vendor-neutral and focused on open, security-first practices.

Combined with my experience auditing the protocol and working extensively with fuzzing and formal verification tools, I hope to help ensure Arbitrum continues to operate with the highest possible security standards.
0xef7b13326c7c680735d792b63587096cf1b273.qualifications:
My work focuses on identifying and preventing vulnerabilities in blockchain infrastructure and decentralized applications.

Over the past decade I have performed security reviews across a wide range of systems including: L2 infrastructure, stablecoins, lending protocols, decentralized exchanges, wallet infrastructure and core smart contract systems.

Within Arbitrum specifically, my work has included reviewing multiple generations of the protocol stack.

I was also part of the Trail of Bits team that audited the smart contracts used for this Security Council election, where we identified a low-severity issue during the review. I have also proven experience reviewing governance actions and emergency upgrades for Nitro/Stylus while at TOB, later overseen by the Security Council.

Beyond audits, I contribute to the broader Ethereum security ecosystem through open-source tooling such as echidna and hevm, which are widely used for fuzz testing and formal verification of smart contracts.
0xef7b13326c7c680735d792b63587096cf1b273.projectsInvolved: Security Expert elected by the Arbitrum DAO for the Arbitrum Audit Program

This has given me a personal interest in seeing Arbitrum succeed, as well as wide & deep knowledge of the technology, which I believe puts me in a strong position to help keep the DAO's chains secure.

0xeee3fb3b792c7ddbb6aef0c440fbc621f4d6fe.name: VP Security Labs
0xeee3fb3b792c7ddbb6aef0c440fbc621f4d6fe.motivation:
Certora is motivated to contribute to the security, resilience, and long-term reliability of the Arbitrum ecosystem.

We believe our combination of deep expertise in smart contract security, formal verification, and protocol design provides a valuable perspective to the Security Council. Our work with leading protocols and infrastructure teams across Web3, together with extensive experience in broader cybersecurity domains, allows us to approach risk with both technical rigor and practical judgment.

We view participation in the Security Council as both a responsibility and a commitment to the ecosystem, and we value the opportunity to support Arbitrum’s security and governance.
0xeee3fb3b792c7ddbb6aef0c440fbc621f4d6fe.qualifications:
Certora specializes in advanced security solutions for decentralized systems, combining formal verification, smart contract audits, and governance and protocol design review. Our core technology, the Certora Prover, enables teams to mathematically verify the correctness of their smart contracts, providing a high level of assurance for critical infrastructure.

We have a strong track record of identifying critical vulnerabilities in leading protocols prior to deployment, as well as uncovering fundamental issues in widely used tooling, including Solidity and LLVM. In addition to implementation-level security, we actively contribute to governance and design reviews for major protocols such as Aave, Lido and Uniswap.

Over the past year we helped top-tier protocols develop their platforms from design to deployment including Aave v4, Lido dual Governance and Lido v3.

Over the past year, Elad Erdheim has served on the Arbitrum Security Council as Certora’s representative, providing direct, hands-on experience with the responsibilities and expectations of the role.

Elad Erdheim, VP of Security Labs at Certora, brings over two decades of experience in cybersecurity, including leadership roles focused on threat management and risk assessment. This combination of deep technical expertise and operational security experience enables a disciplined and practical approach to Security Council responsibilities.

Arbitrum continues to be a home for innovation and liquidity, and we're committed to helping build a weirder and safer future here.

0xbf7241a3e47e412bbbca7d4fdb40b0321b0bbb.name: Aragon
0xbf7241a3e47e412bbbca7d4fdb40b0321b0bbb.motivation: Both security, tokenomics, and governance are intertwined and require experienced and pragmatic input. Aragon is the only other “DAO framework” in the industry besides the currently used OZ Governor. We bring a fresh perspective while still deeply understanding Arbitrum’s current tech-stack. We want to work with the most forward thinking and technologically advanced organisations and bring our own expertise to securing one of the most vibrant and important networks.
0xbf7241a3e47e412bbbca7d4fdb40b0321b0bbb.qualifications:
Aragon’s Extensive Security Track Record:
Security is not a feature at Aragon; it is our primary product. As the first-ever DAO framework, we have spent nine years securing billions of dollars across multiple products and networks.

Zero Exploits Since Inception: Since our mainnet launch in 2017, Aragon has never suffered a major hack or loss of funds. We have successfully secured assets for hundreds of projects.

Security-as-a-Service: Aragon has direct experience as security council member for Taiko, Aztec and Spark. We don’t just read code, we participate in economic, tokenomic, and security reviews.

Builders: The best security council members are those that have broad and deep knowledge of technology and business logic. We are builders first, having deployed dozens of protocols and tools securing tens of billions. Our services help fund the products we build which are often used as public goods. We are a team of 20+ innovators who bring different disciplines within our services.

The Security Council requires members who can act decisively under pressure, understand complex technical interactions, and prioritize the safety of user funds above all else.

Experience: We secure some of the world’s largest on-chain treasuries and protocols both from a technical perspective and operational perspective. We are always at the cutting-edge of security technology now actively testing AI-assisted and agent-based tooling to strengthen our and our users code bases.

Neutrality & Integrity: Aragon is a mission-driven project dedicated to neutral, open-source public goods. We bring a “code-as-law” philosophy that ensures decisions are made based on technical merit and the security of the network.

Full-stack: Our team is composed of experts in all types of development, tokenomics, and policy & advocacy. We are a Swiss based entity, with global employees, that are reliable; you don’t just get one independent security council member, you get our entire team.
0xbf7241a3e47e412bbbca7d4fdb40b0321b0bbb.projectsInvolved: Lido, Curve, Taiko, Spark, Morpho, Aztec

0xeeb8886c48490355d6473901e2c1ea03f9cc83.name: Cyfrin | CEO
0xeeb8886c48490355d6473901e2c1ea03f9cc83.entityType: Organization - Patrick Collins is the representative.
0xeeb8886c48490355d6473901e2c1ea03f9cc83.motivation:
We, the Cyfrin team, care about web3. Arbitrum is a massive part of web3, and know Arbitrum helps make this ecosystem fantastic, with their massive amount of transaction volume and limit pushing technology. We want to help protect Arbitrum, hence our application here. 

We believe in what Arbitrum is doing, and want to be here to help.
0xeeb8886c48490355d6473901e2c1ea03f9cc83.qualifications:
Cyfrin does audits and security for many of the worlds top protocols, many on Arbitrum. We are one of the auditors listed in the Arbitrum Audit Program. We do security for groups like MetaMask, Pancakeswap, WLFI, Securitize and more. We are already security council members on ZKsync, Optimism, Story Protocol, and more, with over $50B in TVL secured. We build open sourced tooling like Aderyn (static analysis), ZKsync Verification (a security council tool), wise-signer (transaction legibility), localsafe (local edition of Safe UI), and more. We have the most watched web3 security curriculum on earth, which has also taught many LLMs, across domains like formal verification, assembly, stateful fuzzing, and more.

Patrick Collins, our representative, has deployed countless educational and production smart contracts on Arbitrum, and onboarded many developers to the L2.
0xeeb8886c48490355d6473901e2c1ea03f9cc83.projectsInvolved: Matter Labs, Optimism, Linea, other L2s

0x7028169f91a6061d5d5d7e4b9b5d91ee4b8171.name: Chief Information Officer
0x7028169f91a6061d5d5d7e4b9b5d91ee4b8171.entityType: The candidancy represents Nethermind as an organization with Mateusz Jędrzejewski serving as the individual representative on the Security Council.
0x7028169f91a6061d5d5d7e4b9b5d91ee4b8171.motivation: Nethermind's security team already evaluates emergency upgrades and responds to cross-protocol incidents as a core function, the Security Council role is a natural extension of work we do every day. Nethermind delegates a candidate, because Arbitrum deserves council members who bring battle-tested incident response discipline, operational security rigor and genuine 24/7 emergency availability, backed by an organization with 400+ smart contract audits and formal verification depth behind them.
0x7028169f91a6061d5d5d7e4b9b5d91ee4b8171.qualifications: Mateusz leads the infrastructure operations and organizational security at Nethermind across many blockchain projects and chain protocols, with direct responsibility for incident response coordination, key management protocols and cross-team emergency triage under time pressure.
0x7028169f91a6061d5d5d7e4b9b5d91ee4b8171.projectsInvolved: Nethermind works with many projects and, in fact, it is because of that work that Mateusz has the relevant experience he does. However, when doing his work Mateusz would always respect your conflict of interest policy, and he would also respect Nethermind's conflict of interest policy - which is largely with coterminous with yours, but if it takes a more conservative position on a conflict, Mateusz will respect that more conservative requirement.
